input.title: Nano-island-encapsulated cobalt single-atom catalysts for breaking activity-stability trade-off in Fenton-like reactions
input.abstract: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have been increasingly acknowledged for their performance in sustainable Fenton-like catalysis. However, SACs face a trade-off between activity and stability in peroxymonosulfate (PMS)-based systems. Herein, we design a nano-island encapsulated single cobalt atom (Co<sub>SA/Zn.O</sub>-ZnO) catalyst to enhance the activity and stability of PMS activation for contaminant degradation via an "island-sea" synergistic effect. In this configuration, small carrier-based ZnO nanoparticles (the "islands") are utilized to confine and stabilize Co single atoms. The expansive ZnO substrate (the "sea") upholds a neutral microenvironment within the reaction system. The Co<sub>SA/Zn.O</sub>-ZnO/PMS system exhibits a remarkable selectivity in exclusively generating sulfate radicals (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>•-</sup>), leading to a complete removal of various recalcitrant pollutants within a shorter period. Characterized by minimal leaching of active sites, robust catalytic performance, and low-toxicity decontamination, this system proves highly efficient in multiple treatment cycles and complex water matrices. The design effectively breaks the activity-stability trade-off typically associated with SACs.

terms.evidence: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have been increasingly acknowledged for their performance in sustainable Fenton-like catalysis.
Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have been increasingly acknowledged for their performance in sustainable Fenton-like catalysis.
However, SACs face a trade-off between activity and stability in peroxymonosulfate (PMS)-based systems.

id: High-entropy alloy enables multi-path electron syn

input.title: High-entropy alloy enables multi-path electron synergism and lattice oxygen activation for enhanced oxygen evolution activity
input.abstract: Electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is key to several energy technologies but suffers from low activity. Leveraging the lattice oxygen activation mechanism (LOM) is a strategy for boosting its activity. However, this approach faces significant thermodynamic challenges, requiring high-valent oxidation of metal ions without compromising their stability. We reveal that high-entropy alloys (HEAs) can efficiently activate the LOM through synergistic multi-path electron transfer. Specifically, the oxidation of nickel is enhanced by this electron transfer, aided by the integration of weaker Co-O bonds, enabling effective LOM at the Ni-Co dual-site. These insights allow the design of a NiFeCoCrW<sub>0.2</sub> HEA that exhibits improved activity, achieving an overpotential of 220 mV at a current density of 10 mA cm<sup>-2</sup>. It also demonstrates good stability, maintaining the potential with less than 5% variation over 90 days at 100 mA cm<sup>-2</sup> current density. This study sheds light on the synergistic effects that confer high activity in HEAs and contribute to the advancement of high-performance OER electrocatalysts.

terms.evidence: Electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is key to several energy technologies but suffers from low activity.
Leveraging the lattice oxygen activation mechanism (LOM) is a strategy for boosting its activity.
We reveal that high-entropy alloys (HEAs) can efficiently activate the LOM through synergistic multi-path electron transfer.

input.title: MXene-Assisted NiFe sulfides for high-performance anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis
input.abstract: Anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis is vital for future large-scale green hydrogen production, however enduring a huge challenge that lacks high-stable oxygen evolution reaction electrocatalysts. Herein, we report a robust OER electrocatalyst for AEMSE by integrating MXene (Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>) with NiFe sulfides ((Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>). The strong interaction between (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub> and Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> induces electron distribution to trigger lattice oxygen mechanism, improving the intrinsic activity, and particularly prohibits the dissolution of Fe species during OER process via the Ti-O-Fe bonding effectively, achieving notable stability. Furthermore, the good retention of sulfates and the abundant groups of Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> provide effective Cl<sup>-</sup> resistance. Accordingly, (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> achieves high OER activity (1.598 V@2 A cm<sup>-2</sup>) and long-term durability (1000 h) in seawater system. Furthermore, AEMSE with industrial current density (0.5 A cm<sup>-2</sup>) and durability (500 h) is achieved by (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> anode and Raney Ni cathode with electrolysis efficiency of 70% and energy consumption of 48.4 kWh kg<sup>-1</sup> H<sub>2</sub>.

relations.evidence_sentence: The strong interaction between (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub> and Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> induces electron distribution to trigger lattice oxygen mechanism, improving the intrinsic activity, and particularly prohibits the dissolution of Fe species during OER process via the Ti-O-Fe bonding effectively, achieving notable stability.

relations.head: Raney Ni阴极
relations.relation_detail: Raney Ni阴极 corresponds_to 电解效率70%
relations.evidence_sentence: 5 A cm<sup>-2</sup>) and durability (500 h) is achieved by (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> anode and Raney Ni cathode with electrolysis efficiency of 70% and energy consumption of 48.

edges.target: 阴离子交换膜海水电解
edges.relation_detail: 氧析出反应电催化剂 corresponds_to 阴离子交换膜海水电解
edges.evidence: Anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis is vital for future large-scale green hydrogen production, however enduring a huge challenge that lacks high-stable oxygen evolution reaction electrocatalysts.

edges.evidence: The strong interaction between (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub> and Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> induces electron distribution to trigger lattice oxygen mechanism, improving the intrinsic activity, and particularly prohibits the dissolution of Fe species during OER process via the Ti-O-Fe bonding effectively, achieving notable stability.

edges.relation_detail: Raney Ni阴极 corresponds_to 电解效率70%
edges.evidence: 5 A cm<sup>-2</sup>) and durability (500 h) is achieved by (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> anode and Raney Ni cathode with electrolysis efficiency of 70% and energy consumption of 48.

L1.head: 阴离子交换膜海水电解
L1.claim: 阴离子交换膜海水电解面临缺乏高稳定氧析出反应电催化剂的挑战。

L1.head: 氧析出反应电催化剂
L1.claim: 通过整合 ）材料可以提升氧析出反应电催化剂的性能。
）与硫化物 ((,)@)的整合可以显著提升阴离子交换膜海水电解的性能，需要材料科学领域的进一步支持以优化材料协同效应。

L2.claim: ） 整合形成 硫化物 ((,)@)

L2.head: 硫化物 ((,)@)
L2.claim: 硫化物 ((,)@) 通过形成 键

L2.head: 键
L2.claim: 键 增强 长期耐久性 (1000小时)
） 整合形成 硫化物 ((,)@)

L2.head: 硫化物 ((,)@)
L2.claim: 硫化物 ((,)@) 触发 晶格氧机制

L2.head: 晶格氧机制
L2.claim: 晶格氧机制 提升 活性 (1.598 @2 )

L3.claim: ） 整合形成复合材料 硫化物 ((,)@)

L3.head: 硫化物 ((,)@)
L3.relation: 通过化学键稳定铁物种
L3.claim: 硫化物 ((,)@) 通过化学键稳定铁物种 键

L3.head: 键
L3.claim: 键 实现电解稳定性提升 长期耐久性 (1000小时)
） 提供电子传导网络 硫化物 ((,)@)

L3.head: 硫化物 ((,)@)
L3.claim: 硫化物 ((,)@) 激活高效反应路径 晶格氧机制

L3.head: 晶格氧机制
L3.claim: 晶格氧机制 降低反应过电位 活性 (1.598 @2 )
